topics.subject: dbms
topics.display_name: Advanced ER Concepts (Weak Entities, Aggregation, Hierarchies)

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: The relational model is the first formal topic after DBMS introduction.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Conceptual design is motivated by the need to produce relational schemas.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: ER modeling is the primary conceptual design tool.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Key and participation constraints are defined on ER structures.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Weak entities, aggregation, and hierarchies build on base ER constraints.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Mapping rules must handle all ER constructs including advanced ones.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: The target of ER-to-relational mapping is the relational model.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Relational algebra formalizes operations on relations.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: DDL creates structures expressed in the relational model.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Integrity constraints are declared within DDL schema definitions.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: ER-level constraints map to relational integrity constraints.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Normalization is defined over relations and functional dependencies.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Refinement decisions depend on preserving integrity constraints.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Normalization often refines schemas produced by ER-to-relational mapping.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: SQL query semantics align with relational algebra operations.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Querying assumes familiarity with schema definitions.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Subqueries and set operations extend core SQL querying patterns.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Trigger bodies contain SQL queries and DML statements.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Triggers often enforce complex integrity conditions beyond declarative constraints.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Views are defined through SQL query expressions.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Updatable view behavior is constrained by integrity rules.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Storage structures implement the relational model physically.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Index design is motivated by query performance optimization.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Transaction behavior is understood through query and update workloads.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Recovery and locking mechanisms depend on storage-level concepts like WAL and page I/O.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Application code embeds and executes SQL queries.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Application logic must handle transaction boundaries and error recovery.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Web architectures extend database-backed application development patterns.

validated_edges.subject: dbms
validated_edges.rationale: Distributed web systems still rely on transaction guarantees.
